channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.description: Blessings Chidakwa in GWERU Seven little coffins, one nation in pain. THIS was the scene at Gweru Sports Club yesterday, where heart-wrenching moments unfolded as the visibly pained and emotionally... (via H-metro) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: H-metro

item.title: Hartzell School Officials Arrested After Two ECD Learners Drown in Disused Swimming Pool
item.description: Hartzell School Tragedy: Officials Arrested After Two ECD Learners Drown Two officials from Hartzell Central Primary School in Penhalonga, Manicaland, have been arrested after two Early Childhood Development (ECD) learners drowned in a swimming pool at the school. The tragic incident occurred on 11 June 2026, with police confirming that the pair now face culpable [...] The post Hartzell School Officials Arrested After Two ECD Learners Drown in Disused Swimming Pool appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Man drowns during photo shoot @bulawayo24
item.description: A sightseeing trip to one of Zimbabwe’s most popular tourist attractions ended in tragedy after a 22-year-old man drowned at Bridal Veil Falls in Chimanimani National Park.The victim, Nyasha Mashava o... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: How does a whole cabinet agree that domestic workers deserve a measly US$90? @nehandaradio @TendaiMbofana
item.description: Social justice advocate Tendai Ruben Mbofana argues that Zimbabwe’s new US$90 minimum wage for domestic workers is not a victory for labour rights but a state-sanctioned endorsement of poverty. The post How does a whole cabinet agree that domestic workers deserve a measly US$90? appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Police Arrest 46 Suspects After Raid on Illegal Alcohol Brewery in Harare @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: Police Arrest 46 Suspects After Raid on Illegal Alcohol Brewery in Harare A major police operation in Harare’s Southerton industrial area has resulted in the arrest of 46 people following the discovery of an alleged illegal alcohol manufacturing facility producing alcoholic beverages using ethanol. The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) said the suspects include 42 Zimbabwean [...] The post Police Arrest 46 Suspects After Raid on Illegal Alcohol Brewery in Harare appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: National Culture, Power Distance and the Zimbabwean State: How Power Centralisation Weakened the Bond Between Citizens and Nationhood
item.description: International sporting tournaments like the ongoing FIFA World Cup 2026 often reveal far more than athletic ability. They provide a window into the deeper social, cultural, and political structures that shape nations. When footballers stand shoulder to shoulder singing their national anthems, observers are witnessing more than patriotism. They are seeing the visible expression of [...] The post National Culture, Power Distance and the Zimbabwean State: How Power Centralisation Weakened the Bond Between Citizens and Nationhood appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Zimbabwe Repatriates Over 600 Citizens Fleeing Xenophobic Violence in South Africa
item.description: Zimbabwe Repatriates Over 600 Citizens Fleeing Xenophobic Violence in South Africa Zimbabwe has repatriated almost 700 citizens from South Africa this month as authorities intensify efforts to assist nationals affected by growing anti-immigrant tensions in parts of the neighbouring country. The assisted voluntary return programme, coordinated by the Government in partnership with international humanitarian organisations, [...] The post Zimbabwe Repatriates Over 600 Citizens Fleeing Xenophobic Violence in South Africa appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Floyd Mayweather Hit With $200K Fraud Charges as 50 Cent Offers Unexpected Help
item.description: Floyd Mayweather is facing renewed legal and financial scrutiny after being hit with felony charges linked to an alleged $200,000 bad cheque used to purchase a luxury timepiece in Las Vegas, a case that has now drawn an unexpected public response from longtime rival-turned-commentator 50 Cent. According to AllHipHop, Mayweather is accused of writing a [...] The post Floyd Mayweather Hit With $200K Fraud Charges as 50 Cent Offers Unexpected Help appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: THE KRUGER NATIONAL PARK HIT: Why Mutare Has Become the Secret Hideout for Cross-Border Murder Suspects
item.description: The geography of Crooks’ Corner is as treacherous as its history. Located at the extreme northern tip of South Africa’s Kruger National Park, it is the precise point where the borders of South Africa, Zimbabwe, and Mozambique converge. For decades, this confluence of the Limpopo and Luvuvhu rivers was a lawless sanctuary for ivory poachers [...] The post THE KRUGER NATIONAL PARK HIT: Why Mutare Has Become the Secret Hideout for Cross-Border Murder Suspects first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
